MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kelly Dixon, via Zoom, for Evered Cohen, Administrator; Danzel Cohen, via Zoom, in propria persona, Objector.

The court queries Ms. Dixon regarding whether she is aware of any objections. Mr. Cohen advises that he objects and offers advocacy for his position. The court reminds the parties that this matter has been pending for nine (9) years. Mr. Cohen reiterates his position and asks that the court consider his objection. The court reiterates its position regarding the time that it has taken to litigate this matter. Ms. Dixon reports of speaking with Mr. Cohen prior to this hearing and of offering to file his papers. Mr. Cohen confirms that he is looking for the distribution that was due to him two (2) years ago and of not receiving notice of prior matters because he had moved. Ms. Dixon advises that what Mr. Cohen is requesting is set forth in the instant petition. The court reminds Mr. Cohen that if he moves, it is his responsibility to provide notice of his new address. Ms. Dixon reports that she has Mr. Cohen's current address, and that proper notice has been provided to all parties in this matter and that the Estate is in the position to be closed.

The matter is taken under submission. The status of accounting set for Oct-25-2022 is taken off calendar in open court.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kelly Dixon, Esq., counsel for Evered Cohen, Administrator (appearing by video conferencing).

The Court reports that this matter is on calendar for the status of closing the estate and queries counsel as to the want of a petition to extend estate administration or to close the estate. Ms. Dixon reports on the status of an appeal filed by Mary Cohen, a daughter of the decedent, filed in August of 2018, and contends that due to the depleted assets of the estate, she elected to appear today rather that incur the added expense of a petition.
The Court takes issues with the description of the estate as "depleted." After discussion, the Court continues the matter to September 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court orders Ms. Dixon to file a Petition to extend estate administration or to close the estate. Courtesy copies of the Petition are to be delivered to Dept. 204 on or before September 1, 2020, 12:00 p.m. The Court further orders that if no petition has been filed and copies served as ordered, the Court will assess sanctions of $50.00 per day against both Ms. Dixon and the administrator of the estate until the Petition is filed. Ms. Dixon acknowledges the terms of the order.

Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kelly Dixon for and with Evered Cohen (Administrator).
Edward Koplowitz Litigation Counsel for Evered Cohen.
Frank Lance Cohen in Propria Persona.

Frank Cohen stated that he is here to object, that he lives at the home, that he feels there is an over valuation and that his Father did not have $1,000,000.00 in coins. Frank Cohen also stated that he does not want a lien on the house, that he and his Mother are disabled and that the house is homesteaded. Ms. Dixon stated that it appears Mary and Lance are co-owners, that the only issue is the property in Texas, and that it either has to be sold or abandoned, that other family members are interested in purchasing it, but they don't want to bid until they know what their distribution is. Ms. Dixon also stated that she is hoping to adjudicate all issues in San Francisco, that the family members want to keep the Texas property in the family, that it is in Bishop's name, that he inherited it from his parents and that she just wants to get the Beneficiaries their money. The Court stated that it does not think this can be a final distribution because there are too many things out there, and suggested doing a first accounting with a preliminary distribution. Mr. Koplowitz raised the issue of his fees being paid. Ms. Dixon stated that she is willing to deem this as a first accounting for preliminary distribution. The First and Final Report and Account of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

The Court granted the petition for appointment of Everend Cohen as Adminstrator with limited authority (subject to the order being revised). Bond is set at $300,000.00. The Court also explained to the Parties that nothing could be done in the Estate without Court approval.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
